title: fraxinus angustifolia subsp. syriaca
intro: Fraxinus angustifolia subsp. syriaca, also known as the Syrian ash, is a subspecies of the narrow-leaved ash tree native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus angustifolia subsp. syriaca include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Fraxinus angustifolia subsp. syriaca can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
